title: China-to-Pakistan BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.target_requirement: Pakistan acceptance of China GB evidence must be confirmed by the exact PSQCA, importer, tender, customs, utility, fire, insurer, or contract route. Lithium BESS is not clearly established from public PSQCA sources as a whole-product compulsory PSQCA item, so do not describe GB 44240-2024 as automatic Pakistan market approval.
target_standards: Pakistan PSQCA, customs, utility, fire, transport, and project requirements applicable to the exact shipment or site
rows.china_equivalent: GB 44240-2024 is a China GB standard for lithium-ion cells and batteries used in electric energy storage. It is China-side mandatory where its scope applies, but it is not a Pakistan approval instrument.
rows.gap: Map GB 44240-2024 clauses to the Pakistan-requested IEC, PSQCA, fire, transport, grid, or project clauses, and mark which items are technical evidence rather than local approval.

mandatory_status.target: Not confirmed as a blanket Pakistan whole-product BESS compulsory certification; mandatory only where the Pakistan route, authority, importer, customs process, tender, insurer, utility, or contract requires or accepts the mapped evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory where GB 44240-2024 scope applies in China

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use GB 44240-2024 as China battery-safety baseline evidence, not as automatic Pakistan or PSQCA whole-product BESS approval.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation Standard Public Service Platform

rows.target_requirement: Pakistan-side reviewers may request IEC 62619, a Pakistan Standard, tender evidence, or project-specific battery evidence. GB/T 36276-2023 can support a technical comparison, but should not be described as a Pakistan Standard or compulsory Pakistan route unless adoption or acceptance is confirmed.
target_standards: IEC 62619 where requested
Pakistan Standard, tender, contract, or project battery requirement where confirmed
rows.china_equivalent: GB/T 36276-2023 is China-side recommended national standard evidence for lithium-ion batteries for electric energy storage and is commonly used in BESS technical files.
rows.gap: Confirm edition, laboratory accreditation, product identity, sample configuration, report language, and whether Pakistan requires IEC evidence, a Pakistan Standard, or a mapped GB/T report.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary or project-required unless a Pakistan route specifically requires or accepts GB/T 36276-2023 evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Recommended and commonly used for China BESS lithium battery evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 36276-2023 is useful China lithium-battery evidence, but Pakistan acceptance must be confirmed for the exact route.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation Standard Public Service Platform

rows.target_requirement: Pakistan grid and project reviewers may need BESS system evidence for PCS, EMS, BMS, operating modes, commissioning, and grid interface. GB/T 36558-2023 can support the system file, but it does not replace NEPRA, utility, owner, insurer, or local site approval.
target_standards: NEPRA and utility project requirements where applicable
IEC 62933 where requested
rows.china_equivalent: GB/T 36558-2023 is China-side recommended national standard evidence for electrochemical energy storage system technical requirements.
rows.gap: Create a system matrix linking China test evidence to Pakistan site design, utility settings, operating modes, commissioning acceptance tests, and unresolved IEC or project gaps.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific unless a Pakistan authority, utility, tender, contract, insurer, or project reviewer requires GB/T 36558-2023 or accepts it as equivalent evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Recommended and commonly used for China BESS system evidence; may become contractually required in China grid-project review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 36558-2023 helps explain the China system baseline but cannot substitute for Pakistan grid or project approval.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation Standard Public Service Platform

rows.requirement: Site Fire-Safety and Emergency-Response Due Diligence
rows.target_requirement: Pakistan public sources do not support a blanket, single national lithium BESS fire-approval route for every project. Treat fire safety as project and local-authority due diligence: confirm the building, electrical, municipal, utility, insurer, EPC, owner, and local fire-service requirements for the actual site before shipment and commissioning.
target_standards: Site-specific building, electrical, municipal, fire-service, insurer, EPC, owner, and utility requirements where applicable
Project fire-safety specification and emergency-response plan
rows.china_equivalent: China evidence under GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, and GB/T 36558-2023 can support hazard analysis, but it is not a Pakistan local-authority fire approval. Translate product evidence into site drawings, spacing, access, ventilation, detection, suppression, shutdown, signage, and emergency-response documents.
rows.gap: Merged former fire-1, fire-3, fire-4, and fire-5: prepare one site package covering installation arrangement, thermal-runaway mitigation, emergency shutdown and isolation, fire-service interface, O&M limits, incident records, and damaged-battery handling.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by the site authority, utility, insurer, EPC, owner, permit, or contract; otherwise project/local-authority due diligence rather than a confirmed blanket Pakistan BESS fire code
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on China project approval, construction/fire review, grid review, owner requirements, and the applicable GB standard scope

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not present PEC or generic PSQCA pages as a lithium BESS site fire approval. Confirm project and local-authority fire-safety requirements for the actual Pakistan installation.

source.publisher: Pakistan Standards and Quality Control Authority (PSQCA) — compulsory-item scope page used only as a scope-screening reference, not as proof of whole-site fire approval

rows.target_requirement: NEPRA regulates Pakistan electricity activities such as licensing, tariff, monitoring, and consumer-protection matters. A BESS project should first classify its role: behind-the-meter backup, solar-plus-storage, merchant storage, utility asset, mini-grid, wheeling, or ancillary-service asset can lead to different NEPRA, utility, tariff, metering, and dispatch treatment.
target_standards: Regulation of Generation, Transmission and Distribution of Electric Power Act, 1997
NEPRA licensing, tariff, monitoring, and regulatory framework as applicable to the project role
rows.china_equivalent: China grid approvals and GB-standard system reports do not create a NEPRA approval. They can support a technical file only after the Pakistan commercial and regulatory role is defined.
rows.gap: Confirm whether the BESS is equipment only, a customer backup system, a generation or supply asset, or a grid-service resource before assuming whether NEPRA approval is mandatory or project-specific.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the BESS performs a regulated electricity activity requiring NEPRA licence, registration, tariff, approval, dispatch, or metering treatment; project-specific for purely customer-side equipment
mandatory_status.cn: China grid approval obligations depend on project type, voltage level, market role, and grid-connection contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Determine the Pakistan electricity-market role of the BESS before claiming a NEPRA licence, tariff, or approval requirement.

rows.requirement: Utility Interconnection, Grid-Code Review, and Site Commissioning
rows.target_requirement: A grid-connected Pakistan BESS should be reviewed with the relevant distribution company, transmission company, or project grid operator for protection, metering, anti-islanding, fault ride-through, voltage/frequency limits, harmonics, communications, dispatch, and commissioning tests. Pakistan and China both use 50 Hz systems, but voltage level, grounding, protection philosophy, utility settings, grid strength, ambient conditions, and communications remain site-specific.
target_standards: Pakistan grid code and utility interconnection requirements where applicable
Project commissioning and acceptance tests
rows.china_equivalent: GB/T 36558-2023 system evidence is useful because the frequency basis is similar, but China settings are not a Pakistan interconnection approval. PCS firmware, transformer taps, earthing, relay settings, harmonic limits, HVAC derating, SCADA, and remote monitoring should be validated for the Pakistan point of connection.
rows.gap: Merged former grid-5 into grid-2: produce Pakistan-specific relay settings, grid model, power-quality study, short-circuit study, SCADA protocol, commissioning plan, and site acceptance records.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected BESS where utility interconnection, grid-code, EPC, owner, insurer, or commissioning approval applies; details are project and connection-point specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or contract-specific for China grid-connected projects through grid-company technical review, connection agreement, and commissioning acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A China grid report is not a Pakistan interconnection approval. The 50 Hz match helps engineering review but does not remove site-specific utility commissioning.

source.publisher: National Electric Power Regulatory Authority (NEPRA) — legal and code documents page; utility-specific interconnection documents must be obtained for the project

rows.requirement: PSQCA Scope Screening for Lithium BESS and Components
rows.target_requirement: PSQCA publishes Pakistan Standards, adopted ISO/IEC standards, and compulsory-item information. Public sources do not clearly establish lithium BESS as a whole-product compulsory PSQCA item. Screen the exact shipment by HS code, product family, and bill of materials because cells, modules, PCS, transformers, switchgear, cables, plugs, protective devices, or other electrical accessories may have separate Pakistan Standard or certification routes.
target_standards: PSQCA Act 1996
PSQCA adopted ISO/IEC standards list
PSQCA list of compulsory items to meet Pakistan Standards
Pakistan Standard, tender, customs, importer, inspection-body, or authority route where confirmed
rows.china_equivalent: China GB evidence under GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, and GB/T 36558-2023 can support a technical file but does not by itself prove PSQCA adoption, compulsory status, or acceptance for the exact BESS or component.
rows.gap: Merged former PSQCA route, compulsory-item screening, adoption, and recognition-limit rows: obtain written confirmation on whether the shipment needs a PS mark, Pakistan Standard evidence, direct IEC report, mapped China GB report, recognized-lab report, or only project due-diligence documents.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only for products or components that fall under PSQCA compulsory-item scope, a Pakistan Standard, customs route, tender, contract, inspection-body decision, or authority requirement; lithium BESS as a whole product is not clearly confirmed as compulsory from public PSQCA sources
mandatory_status.cn: China GB evidence may be mandatory or recommended depending on the exact standard and product scope; it remains China-side evidence unless the Pakistan route accepts it

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat PSQCA as a scope-confirmation gate for Pakistan BESS imports. Do not claim lithium BESS whole-product compulsory PSQCA approval unless the exact route confirms it.

source.publisher: Pakistan Standards and Quality Control Authority (PSQCA)

rows.target_requirement: IEC 62619:2022 is the current IEC safety standard for secondary lithium cells and batteries for industrial applications, and IEC 62933 is the IEC electrical energy storage system series. In Pakistan BESS files, these are normally project, tender, insurer, EPC, owner, inspection-body, or PSQCA-route evidence only when the exact part and edition are called up.
target_standards: IEC 62933 series
IEC 62933-5-2 where system-safety evidence is requested
rows.china_equivalent: Map GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 to IEC 62619 where battery safety is requested, and map GB/T 36558-2023 to the exact IEC 62933 part where system-level evidence is requested.
rows.gap: Confirm the accepted title, edition, report language, laboratory accreditation, certification mark if any, sample representativeness, and whether the reviewer wants a certificate, a test report, or a clause-level technical crosswalk.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary or project-required unless PSQCA adoption, tender, contract, insurer, utility, inspection body, or authority route calls up the exact IEC part and edition
mandatory_status.cn: Usually additional export or project evidence; China mandatory status depends on the GB standard scope, while GB/T standards are recommended unless incorporated by project or contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62619 and IEC 62933 are useful evidence families, but Pakistan BESS compliance depends on the exact adopted standard, direct IEC acceptance, tender, contract, or project-review route.

source.publisher: Pakistan Standards and Quality Control Authority (PSQCA)

rows.target_requirement: Lithium cells and batteries shipped to Pakistan should have UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, Part III, subsection 38.3 evidence, including the test summary made available through the transport chain. The evidence must match the shipped design type; material changes to cell supplier, chemistry, capacity, protection circuit, module layout, enclosure, or process should be reviewed before relying on an older report.
target_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, Part III, subsection 38.3
UN 38.3 test-summary availability through the transport chain
rows.china_equivalent: China GB safety reports under GB 44240-2024 or GB/T 36276-2023 are product-safety evidence, not dangerous-goods transport evidence. Export files should reconcile China design-change records with the UN 38.3 tested design and the actual Pakistan-bound shipment.
rows.gap: Merged former transport-1, transport-2, and transport-5: verify the report, test summary, manufacturer and model identity, design revision, and change-control bridge before booking. Classification, packing, SDS, and emergency information remain logistics documents for the actual route and carrier.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for lithium battery dangerous-goods transport where UN model rules and modal air, sea, road, port, carrier, customs, or freight-forwarder practice apply
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory in practice for China export transport acceptance where lithium batteries are offered for transport under dangerous-goods rules

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Pakistan market-access evidence and China GB safety reports do not replace UN 38.3 transport evidence for lithium batteries.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
